title: Clockify vs Kimai for Power users
meta_description: Compare Clockify vs Kimai for Power users. Kimai wins when you need self-hosting and deep customization of your tracking system.
constraint_lens: You need a time tracking tool that you can fully control and customize without hitting limits.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for building a fully customizable time tracking system with self-hosting and full data control.
one_second_verdict.reason: Clockify fails first because it runs as a hosted service without self-hosting or deep system-level customization.

verdict.summary: Kimai is the better choice when you want full control over your time tracking system. It can be self-hosted and customized at the system level, including how data is stored and managed. Clockify runs as a hosted service with fixed structure and limited customization, which means you cannot extend or control the system beyond its built-in features.
verdict.decision_rule: If the tool does not allow self-hosting or deep customization of the tracking system, Clockify fails first.

sections.heading: Why Kimai fits full system control
sections.content: You want to control how your time tracking system works, including hosting and customization. Kimai can be installed on your own server and modified to fit your workflow. Clockify is a hosted tool, which means you use the system as provided and cannot change its structure or hosting environment.

bullets.point: The tool can be self-hosted on your own server or infrastructure.
bullets.why_it_matters: This gives you full control over your data and how the system is run.

bullets.point: The system can be customized at the server and application level.
bullets.why_it_matters: This allows you to adjust workflows and features beyond standard limits.

bullets.point: Data storage and access are fully controlled by your own environment.
bullets.why_it_matters: This removes dependency on external services and allows deeper customization.

bullets.point: The tool is ready to use as a hosted service with no setup required.
bullets.why_it_matters: This makes it easy to start, but limits control over the system.

bullets.point: Time tracking features are available immediately after signing in.
bullets.why_it_matters: This removes setup effort, but prevents deep customization.

bullets.point: All infrastructure and maintenance are handled automatically.
bullets.why_it_matters: This reduces effort, but removes the ability to control or modify the system.

items.fails_when: Kimai becomes difficult to manage if you do not want to handle server setup or maintenance.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Clockify if you want a ready-to-use tool with no hosting responsibilities.

items.fails_when: Clockify breaks when you need self-hosting or deeper customization beyond built-in features.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Kimai when you want full control over your system.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This verdict might flip if you prefer a tool that works immediately without managing servers or customization. In that case, Clockify can be better despite its limitations.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Choose Kimai if you want self-hosting and full system control.
Choose Kimai if you need deep customization beyond standard features.
Choose Clockify if you want a ready-to-use tool with no setup.

faqs.q: Why is Kimai better for Power users?
faqs.a: Because it allows self-hosting and deep customization of the time tracking system.
No, it runs as a hosted service without self-hosting options.
Yes, it requires installing and managing the system on your own server.

faqs.q: When would a Power user still choose Clockify?
faqs.a: A Power user might choose Clockify if they want a simple tool without managing hosting or customization.
